zh.title: GPT-5.4 发布：重新评估你的选型策略
zh.subtitle: 首个大一统前沿模型，原生计算机操控 + 百万上下文窗口
zh.tldr: OpenAI 发布 GPT-5.4，整合推理、编码、Agent 和计算机操控能力于一体，Tool Search 机制使 Agent 调用成本降低 47%，API 已开放，开发者应立即评估迁移价值。

keyMetrics.subject: GPT-5.4

rows.note: GPT-5.4 Pro 数据，GPT-5.2 Pro 为 54.2%

keyMetrics.footnote: Claude Opus 4.6 和 Gemini 3 Pro 的 benchmark 数据来自各自官方发布，口径可能不完全一致；GPT-5.4 benchmark 来自 OpenAI 官方博客

zh.whatHappened: 2026 年 3 月 5 日，OpenAI 发布 GPT-5.4，定位为「首个大一统前沿模型」，提供三个版本：标准版、Thinking（推理增强）和 Pro（最高性能）。核心升级包括：**原生计算机操控能力**（OSWorld 得分 75%，超过人类基线 72.4%）、**Tool Search 机制**（按需查找工具定义，Agent 场景 token 消耗降低 47%）、**1M token 上下文窗口**（OpenAI 最大）。Thinking 版本会展示初始推理计划，用户可中途调整方向。标准版 API 定价 $2.50/$15 per 1M tokens（input/output），较 GPT-5.2 的 $1.75/$14 有所上调。GPT-5.2 Thinking 将于 2026 年 6 月 5 日下线。
zh.whyItMatters: 这是 OpenAI 首次在单一模型中整合编码、推理、Agent 工作流和计算机操控四大能力，意味着开发者不再需要为不同任务切换模型。**Tool Search 带来的 47% token 节省**对构建复杂 Agent 系统的团队影响最大——如果你的应用注册了大量工具，调用成本将显著下降。OSWorld 超越人类基线意味着自动化 GUI 操作场景（RPA、测试自动化）进入实用阶段。但需注意，input 单价从 $1.75 上调至 $2.50（+43%），简单调用场景成本可能不降反升。

groups.name: AI Agent 开发者
groups.description: Tool Search 机制大幅降低多工具 Agent 的 token 消耗；原生计算机操控能力开启新的自动化场景；需评估是否从 GPT-5.2 迁移

groups.name: 个人开发者 / 独立开发者
groups.description: 统一模型降低了选型复杂度，一个 API 覆盖推理+编码+Agent；但 input 单价上涨 43%，需根据使用模式计算实际成本变化

groups.name: 企业 AI 平台团队
groups.description: GPT-5.2 Thinking 6月下线，需要制定迁移计划；Pro 版本高昂定价（$30/$180）适合高价值任务但需严格控制调用量

groups.name: RPA / 测试自动化团队
groups.description: 计算机操控超越人类基线，GUI 自动化场景从概念验证进入生产可用阶段

immediate: 在 OpenAI Playground 用 GPT-5.4 跑你现有应用的典型 prompt，对比 GPT-5.2 的输出质量和 token 消耗
检查你的 Agent 系统注册了多少工具——如果超过 20 个，Tool Search 可能为你节省显著成本
查看 OpenAI 官方迁移指南，确认 GPT-5.2 Thinking 的下线时间表（2026-06-05）
thisWeek: 用实际业务数据做 A/B 测试：GPT-5.4 标准版 vs GPT-5.2，计算 input 涨价后的总体成本变化
如果有 RPA 或 GUI 自动化需求，搭建概念验证评估原生计算机操控能力
评估 GPT-5.4 Thinking 的推理透明性是否满足你的合规或审计需求
watchAndWait: 等待第三方独立 benchmark（如 LMSYS Chatbot Arena）验证实际表现，OpenAI 自测数据可能存在偏差
观察 Anthropic 和 Google 是否在未来数周内发布竞品回应
关注社区反馈中 GPT-5.4 在长上下文（>500K tokens）场景下的实际表现

options.name: 立即迁移到 GPT-5.4
options.description: 将现有 GPT-5.2 调用切换到 GPT-5.4，启用 Tool Search 优化
options.suitableFor: Agent 工具数量多（>20）、需要计算机操控能力、或需要更强推理能力的团队
options.cost: input 单价上涨 43%（$1.75->$2.50），但 Tool Search 可抵消部分成本
options.risk: 新模型可能存在边缘场景 regression；已有 prompt 可能需要调优

options.name: 混合部署策略
options.description: 高价值任务用 GPT-5.4/Pro，简单任务继续用 GPT-5.2 或更便宜的模型
options.suitableFor: 成本敏感且任务类型多样的团队
options.cost: 需要维护多模型路由逻辑的工程开销
options.risk: 模型行为差异可能导致用户体验不一致

options.name: 等待竞品对比后再决定
options.description: 等 1-2 周独立评测出炉，同时评估 Claude Opus 4.6 和 Gemini 3 Pro
options.suitableFor: 不急于迁移、希望做充分选型对比的团队
options.cost: 时间成本；可能错过早期采用优势
options.risk: GPT-5.2 Thinking 将在 3 个月后下线，不宜拖太久

options.name: 保持现状
options.description: 继续使用 GPT-5.2/5.3 或其他模型，暂不迁移
options.suitableFor: 当前方案运行良好、成本可控、且不需要计算机操控或 Tool Search 能力的团队
options.risk: GPT-5.2 Thinking 将于 2026-06-05 下线，需在此之前完成迁移规划

risks.title: Input 涨价被忽视
risks.description: 47% 的 Agent 成本节省来自 Tool Search 减少 token 消耗，但 input 单价本身上涨了 43%。如果你的场景不涉及大量工具调用，实际成本可能不降反升。迁移前务必用真实流量做成本测算。

risks.title: Benchmark 数据来自 OpenAI 自测
risks.description: 目前公布的 GDPval、OSWorld 等分数均来自 OpenAI 官方，尚无 LMSYS Arena 等独立第三方验证。历史上模型发布初期的自测数据与真实使用体验存在差距，不应仅凭 benchmark 做迁移决策。

risks.title: 急于全量迁移导致 regression
risks.description: 新模型在特定 prompt 模式或领域知识上可能存在退化。建议先在非关键路径上灰度测试，收集 1-2 周数据后再决定全量切换。

en.title: GPT-5.4 Ships: Reassess Your LLM Strategy Now
en.subtitle: OpenAI's unified model brings computer use, 1M context, and a pricing tradeoff
en.tldr: OpenAI released GPT-5.4, combining reasoning, coding, agents, and native computer use in one model. Tool Search cuts agent token usage by 47%, but input pricing rose 43% vs GPT-5.2. Evaluate before migrating.

keyMetrics.subject: GPT-5.4
keyMetrics.footnote: Claude and Gemini benchmarks from their respective releases; GPT-5.4 benchmarks from OpenAI's official blog. Cross-model comparison may have methodology differences.

en.whatHappened: On March 5, 2026, OpenAI released GPT-5.4 in three variants: standard, Thinking (reasoning-enhanced), and Pro (maximum performance). Key upgrades: **native computer use** (75% on OSWorld, surpassing the 72.4% human baseline), **Tool Search** (on-demand tool definition lookup, cutting agent token consumption by 47%), and **1M token context window** (largest from OpenAI). The Thinking variant shows its reasoning plan upfront, allowing mid-process steering. Standard API pricing is $2.50/$15 per 1M tokens (input/output), up from GPT-5.2's $1.75/$14. GPT-5.2 Thinking will be deprecated on June 5, 2026.
en.whyItMatters: This is OpenAI's first model unifying coding, reasoning, agent workflows, and computer use, eliminating the need to route between specialized models. **The 47% token savings from Tool Search** matters most for teams building complex agent systems with many registered tools. Computer use surpassing human baselines on OSWorld signals that GUI automation (RPA, test automation) is entering production-ready territory. However, the 43% input price increase ($1.75 to $2.50) means simple API call patterns may see higher costs, not lower.

groups.name: AI Agent developers
groups.description: Tool Search dramatically reduces token overhead for multi-tool agents; native computer use unlocks new automation capabilities; migration evaluation from GPT-5.2 is now necessary

groups.name: Solo / indie developers
groups.description: Unified model simplifies selection -- one API for reasoning + coding + agents; but the 43% input price increase requires cost recalculation based on your usage pattern

groups.name: Enterprise AI platform teams
groups.description: GPT-5.2 Thinking deprecation in June requires migration planning; Pro tier pricing ($30/$180) suits high-value tasks but demands strict call volume control

groups.name: RPA / test automation teams
groups.description: Computer use exceeding human baseline moves GUI automation from proof-of-concept to production viability

immediate: Run your existing prompts through GPT-5.4 in OpenAI Playground and compare output quality and token usage against GPT-5.2
Count how many tools your agent system registers -- if over 20, Tool Search likely saves you significant costs
Review the OpenAI migration guide and note the GPT-5.2 Thinking deprecation date (June 5, 2026)
thisWeek: A/B test with real production data: GPT-5.4 standard vs GPT-5.2, calculating total cost impact including the input price increase
If you have RPA or GUI automation needs, build a proof-of-concept using the native computer use capability
Evaluate whether GPT-5.4 Thinking's reasoning transparency meets your compliance or audit requirements
watchAndWait: Wait for independent benchmarks (e.g., LMSYS Chatbot Arena) to validate real-world performance -- OpenAI's self-reported scores may be optimistic
Watch for competitive responses from Anthropic and Google in the coming weeks
Monitor community feedback on GPT-5.4's actual performance with long contexts (>500K tokens)

options.name: Migrate to GPT-5.4 now
options.description: Switch existing GPT-5.2 calls to GPT-5.4, enable Tool Search optimization
options.suitableFor: Teams with many agent tools (>20), needing computer use, or requiring stronger reasoning
options.cost: Input price rises 43% ($1.75 to $2.50), partially offset by Tool Search savings
options.risk: Edge-case regressions in new model; existing prompts may need tuning

options.name: Hybrid deployment
options.description: Use GPT-5.4/Pro for high-value tasks, keep GPT-5.2 or cheaper models for simple tasks
options.suitableFor: Cost-sensitive teams with diverse task types
options.cost: Engineering overhead to maintain multi-model routing logic
options.risk: Behavioral differences between models may cause inconsistent user experience

options.name: Wait for independent benchmarks
options.description: Defer migration 1-2 weeks until third-party evaluations are available; compare with Claude Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3 Pro
options.suitableFor: Teams not under time pressure who want thorough model selection
options.cost: Time cost; may miss early-adopter advantages
options.risk: GPT-5.2 Thinking sunsets in 3 months -- don't wait too long

options.name: Stay on current setup
options.description: Continue using GPT-5.2/5.3 or other models without migrating
options.suitableFor: Teams whose current solution works well and doesn't need computer use or Tool Search
options.risk: GPT-5.2 Thinking will be deprecated on June 5, 2026 -- plan migration before then

risks.title: The 47% savings may not apply to your use case
risks.description: The cost reduction comes from Tool Search reducing token consumption in multi-tool agent scenarios. Meanwhile, input pricing itself rose 43% ($1.75 to $2.50). If your application doesn't use many tools, your actual costs may increase. Always benchmark with real traffic before committing to migration.

risks.title: Benchmarks are self-reported
risks.description: All published GDPval, OSWorld, and other scores come from OpenAI. No independent verification (LMSYS Arena, etc.) is available yet. Historically, self-reported launch benchmarks have diverged from real-world performance. Do not make migration decisions based solely on these numbers.

risks.title: Rushing a full migration may cause regressions
risks.description: New models can regress on specific prompt patterns or domain knowledge areas. Run a canary deployment on non-critical paths for 1-2 weeks before switching production traffic.
